__meta.framing: US stock markets have NOT opened yet today. Figures are the prior session's close (Thursday, June 25). NEVER say "today" for the move — say "closed [that day] at". You may say markets are "looking to open" today, but do NOT predict a direction (no futures data provided).

worldCup.name: Ivory Coast at Curaçao
worldCup.espnNote: A Nicolas Pépé brace powered Ivory Coast to a 2-0 win over Curacao.

worldCup.name: Germany at Ecuador
worldCup.espnNote: Ecuador produced a rousing fightback to come from behind and beat Germany 2-1 in their final Group E match in New Jersey to put themselves into the last 32 of the World Cup.

worldCup.name: Sweden at Japan
worldCup.espnNote: Japan and Sweden booked their places in the knockout phase of the World Cup as they drew 1-1 in their final Group F clash in Dallas.

worldCup.name: Netherlands at Tunisia
worldCup.espnNote: Brian Brobbey scored his third goal of the World Cup, the Netherlands got a pair of goals that deflected off Tunisia into its own net, and the Dutch rolled in the rain to a 3-1 victory Thursday.

worldCup.name: Australia at Paraguay
worldCup.espnNote: Australia and Paraguay played to a 0-0 draw Thursday night in a result that clinched a spot in the knockout round of the World Cup for the Socceroos and will likely be enough for the Paraguayans to advance.

worldCup.name: United States at Türkiye
worldCup.espnNote: Kaan Ayhan scored on the final kick of the match, and Türkiye beat the United States 3-2 Thursday night for its only win of the World Cup.

topStories.headline: Onsemi to buy Synaptics for $7B in all‑stock deal
topStories.whatHappened: Onsemi agreed today to acquire Synaptics in a $7 billion all‑stock transaction.
topStories.whyItMatters: Massive consolidation in chip sector could reshape AI/hardware supply chains and impact broader tech sentiment.
topStories.depth: This isn’t just another merger — Onsemi grabbing Synaptics for $7B shows how hot chip real estate still is. If you're watching hardware stocks or AI gadget supply chains, this could shift the board.
topStories.whatToSay: ‘Onsemi buying Synaptics for $7B — now that’s a hardware flex.’
topStories._selectionReason: Huge tech sector deal today with clear implications for AI/hardware conversation.

topStories.headline: Starlink offers free internet in Venezuela after quake
topStories.whatHappened: Starlink is providing free internet access in Venezuela in response to deadly earthquakes today.
topStories.whyItMatters: Connectivity in disaster zones is life‑critical, and this move shows how private space‑based services can plug gaps fast.
topStories.depth: So SpaceX’s Starlink is back in the headlines — not for rockets this time but hooking up post‑quake Venezuela. Tech doing some literal real‑world good, finally.
topStories.whatToSay: ‘Starlink just went full rescue mode in Venezuela today.’
topStories._selectionReason: Interesting tech/business story with human impact — not typical gadget news.

topStories.headline: US moves ahead with $700M jet‑engine sale to Turkey
topStories.whatHappened: The US approved a $700 million sale of jet engines to Turkey today, despite lawmakers’ objections.
topStories.whyItMatters: This is a significant move in defense relations — impacts NATO dynamics and regional power balances.
topStories.depth: We’re talking jets and geopolitics — $700 million deal with Turkey cleared today. If you care about defence or alliances, this is big.
topStories.whatToSay: ‘US just green‑lit a $700M jet‑engine sale to Turkey today.’
topStories._selectionReason: Major defense/politics news with real global stakes.

topStories.headline: Samsung plans $648B South Korea investment
topStories.whatHappened: Samsung unveiled plans today to invest $648 billion in South Korea, including major chip plant expansion.
topStories.whyItMatters: It’s one of the largest corporate bets ever — feeds into U.S.‑China tech race and domestic chip supply chains.
topStories.depth: Samsung dropping nearly $650 billion on South Korean manufacturing — basically says ‘we’re still king of chips.’
topStories.whatToSay: ‘Samsung just committed $648B at home — chip bets are still on.’
topStories._selectionReason: Massive business/tech investment news with broader economic implications.

topStories.headline: OpenAI leans toward postponing IPO until next year
topStories.whatHappened: OpenAI is reportedly leaning toward delaying its IPO until next year, sources say today.
topStories.whyItMatters: Delay in public debut affects AI market valuations and investor expectations right now.
topStories.depth: OpenAI pushing their IPO to 2027 or whatever again? That’s going to keep AI hype charged — for better or worse.
topStories.whatToSay: ‘Looks like OpenAI is kicking their IPO to next year.’
_context: IPO likely delayed until next year
topStories._selectionReason: High‑interest AI/public markets news with huge cultural attention.
topStories._verificationConcerns: Based on NYT via Reuters — high confidence but still reported indirect.

dynamicSports.whatHappened: Türkiye beat the United States 3–2 in World Cup group play Thursday night, with Kaan Ayhan scoring the winner in the 90th minute plus 8 seconds.
dynamicSports.whyItMatters: The US needed a result to stay alive in knockout contention. Ayhan's last-kick goal means Türkiye claims its only win of the group and advances, while the Americans are eliminated without a path forward in their final match.
dynamicSports.theRead: This is brutal for the USMNT. You don't blow a 2–1 lead in stoppage time at a World Cup and walk away. Türkiye was desperate—this was their only win—and they found it when it mattered most. The US showed fight (two goals, one from own-goal chaos) but couldn't close it out, and that's the difference between a team that moves on and one that doesn't.
ammo: Kaan Ayhan scored in the 90th minute plus 8 seconds—the final kick of the match
Türkiye's 3–2 victory was their only win in group play
The US scored twice but conceded three, including goals in the 10th and 31st minutes
dynamicSports.whatToBringUp: Did you see that? Kaan Ayhan with the last kick of the match—90+8—and Türkiye steals it 3–2. That's the kind of gut-punch finish that ends tournaments.
dynamicSports.ourPick: Türkiye advances because they have one win and will likely have the better tiebreaker picture than any team sitting on one point in their group.

dynamicSports.name: KC @ TB
dynamicSports.label: MLB
dynamicSports.headline: Tampa Bay Rays 13–2 over Kansas City Royals
dynamicSports.facts: Tampa Bay Rays beat Kansas City Royals 13–2
dynamicSports.whatHappened: Tampa Bay demolished Kansas City 13–2 on Thursday.
dynamicSports.whyItMatters: An 11-run margin is a blowout that signals complete pitching control and offensive dominance. Tampa scored in bunches while Kansas City couldn't generate anything meaningful.
dynamicSports.theRead: This is the kind of win that breaks a team's spirit. The Rays came out swinging and didn't let up. Kansas City had no answer at any point. One game doesn't define a season, but when you lose 13–2, the other team's doing everything right and you're doing nothing right.
ammo: Final score: Tampa Bay 13, Kansas City 2
11-run margin of victory
Game played Thursday
dynamicSports.whatToBringUp: Tampa Bay just ran Kansas City off the field 13–2—that's the kind of complete dismantling that doesn't happen by accident.
dynamicSports.ourPick: Tampa Bay looks dangerous right now because they can score in volume and clearly have pitching that keeps opponents shut down, which is the formula for a playoff team.

heroOverride.title: Onsemi to buy Synaptics for $7B in all‑stock deal

copy.title: Onsemi buys Synaptics for $7B. Rays demolish Royals 13–2. Travelers Championship live.
copy.keyTakeaway: Onsemi is acquiring Synaptics for $7 billion in an all-stock deal as chip consolidation accelerates. Tampa Bay routed Kansas City 13–2 in a statement win. The Travelers Championship heads into the final round with Eric Cole holding a one-shot lead at 7-under.

todaysHits.sports: Tampa Bay demolished Kansas City 13–2, exposing the Royals' lineup against a Rays team firing on all cylinders. The gap between contenders and pretenders shows up fast in June, and Kansas City's next few weeks will tell you whether this was a blip or a season-long problem.
todaysHits.markets: Onsemi agreed to acquire Synaptics for $7 billion in an all-stock deal, signaling chipmakers are consolidating around specialized markets instead of competing on price. Meanwhile, Apple fell 6.1% and Microsoft dropped 3.5% as mega-cap tech got hit hard while small caps surged 3.1% on the week — that rotation is the real story.
todaysHits.golf: Eric Cole leads the Travelers Championship at 7-under with Nico Echavarria and Ben Griffin one shot back heading into Sunday's final round.
todaysHits.f1: The Lenovo Austrian Grand Prix at the Red Bull Ring is this weekend — Andrea Kimi Antonelli enters with a 41-point championship lead and a chance to make this title race look like a formality.
todaysHits.worldcup: World Cup 2026 is underway with six matches played so far.
todaysHits.culture: OpenAI is leaning toward postponing its IPO until next year, signaling the company wants more stable financials before going public. Even AI's hottest name doesn't feel the pressure to rush — and that tells you something about where the market actually is right now.

lead.headline: Rays Dismantle Royals 13–2 in Statement Win
lead.whatHappened: The Tampa Bay Rays dominated the Kansas City Royals 13–2 on Friday, turning what looked like a competitive matchup into a blowout by the middle innings.
lead.whyBullet1: The Royals' offense completely vanished — getting outscored 13–2 signals a team that either hit a wall or ran into a Rays squad firing on all cylinders at exactly the wrong time.
lead.whyBullet2: Games this lopsided reset momentum and confidence heading into the weekend, especially in a division race where every series win carries weight.
lead.theRead: The Rays just proved they can dismantle any lineup when their pitching and bats click together. Kansas City came in expecting a fight and got embarrassed instead. This is the kind of game that sticks with a team — not because one loss ends anything, but because it exposes how quickly things spiral when your approach breaks down. For Tampa, it's a statement that they're not coasting through June. For Kansas City, it's a wake-up call that depth matters more than talent on paper.
ammo: Final score: Rays 13, Royals 2
Tampa Bay outscored Kansas City by 11 runs
The Royals managed just 2 runs across 9 innings
lead.whatToSay: Tampa just showed Kansas City that this division isn't theirs to lose anymore.

headlines.head: Onsemi buys Synaptics for $7B — chip consolidation keeps rolling
headlines.sub: All-stock deal signals that semiconductor real estate is still hot even as some chip stocks take a breather. Shows acquirers see value in hardware and AI supply chains right now, even with rates where they are.

headlines.head: Apple cratered 6.1% — worst day in the bunch
headlines.sub: Nasdaq closed flat for the week but Apple's slide dragged down the mega-cap cohort; Microsoft down 3.5% too.

headlines.head: Small caps ripped 3.1% on the week — Russell 2000 is the real winner
headlines.sub: IWM closed up 0.7% today and sits +3.1% for the week, way outpacing the S&P and tech.

markets.mood: Markets split personality today: mega-cap tech got hammered with Apple down 6.1% and Microsoft off 3.5%, but small caps surged 3.1% on the week and semiconductors stayed in deal mode with Onsemi grabbing Synaptics for $7 billion.
markets.whyBullet1: Treasury yields fell 1.6% on the week, which typically signals growth concerns — that's why mega-cap tech stocks carrying high valuations got hit while small caps and beaten-down hardware names found buyers.
markets.whyBullet2: The Onsemi-Synaptics deal at $7 billion shows chip buyers still see runway even as valuations compress — consolidation is cheaper than innovation right now, and the sector is reshuffling around that reality.
markets.bringUp: Apple fell 6.1% while small caps gained 3.1% on the week — that's not noise, that's money rotating out of mega-cap growth into beaten-down cyclicals and domestically-focused businesses, and Onsemi just paid $7 billion to prove chip consolidation is still very much on.
markets.theRead: The Onsemi-Synaptics deal is the clearest signal yet that chip buyers see more value in acquiring specialized assets than building them. But the bigger story is the rotation: when yields drop, high-multiple growth stocks get repriced and value snaps back hard. Apple and Microsoft got caught in that shift. Small caps are the beneficiary because they're cheap, domestic, and don't need rate cuts to stay afloat. AMD gaining 2.5% while Coinbase fell 5.1% shows the market is sorting winners from momentum plays — and that sorting is still in progress.
ammo: Apple closed down 6.1%, worst mega-cap performer of the day
Russell 2000 up 3.1% on the week, vastly outpacing the S&P 500
10-year Treasury yield fell 1.6% on the week
Onsemi acquiring Synaptics for $7 billion in an all-stock deal announced Friday
Microsoft down 3.5%, AMD up 2.5% — a clean rotation signal

golf.headline: Cole Leads Travelers by One Heading Into Sunday
golf.whyCare1: Eric Cole sits at 7-under with Nico Echavarria and Ben Griffin one shot back — a one-shot lead at TPC River Highlands heading into Sunday is about as compressed as a leaderboard gets.
golf.whyCare2: Travelers is a top-tier PGA Tour event known for tight final-round drama, and this leaderboard is set up to deliver exactly that.
golf.watchFor: Whether Cole can hold his nerve on a track where final-round leads evaporate, and which of the players one back makes the decisive move Sunday afternoon.
golf.theRead: Cole has the lead but no cushion. Echavarria and Griffin are right there, and one-shot leads at TPC River Highlands have a way of disappearing fast. This isn't about who's the best player in the field — it's about who doesn't blink when the back nine gets tight on Sunday. Cole has earned the right to be here, but he'll have to earn it again tomorrow.
ammo: Eric Cole leads at 7-under par
Nico Echavarria and Ben Griffin are tied for second at 6-under, one shot back
TPC River Highlands is a par-70 course known for producing tight Sunday finishes
golf.whatToSay: Cole's got the lead, but one shot at Travelers on Sunday is basically nothing — this thing is wide open.

f1.headline: Antonelli Arrives in Austria With a 41-Point Lead
f1.whyCare1: Andrea Kimi Antonelli holds a 41-point championship lead heading into the Lenovo Austrian Grand Prix this weekend — that's roughly a win-and-a-half advantage under the current points system.
f1.whyCare2: Austria's Red Bull Ring is a short, punchy circuit that tends to produce chaos, which is the best thing the chasers have going for them if they want to close the gap.
f1.watchFor: Whether the drivers behind Antonelli can actually put pressure on him, or whether another clean Sunday turns a title race into a coronation.
f1.theRead: A 41-point lead at this stage is not comfortable — it's a signal that one driver has been more consistent, more clinical, and more composed than everyone else in the field. Austria has a history of producing drama, which is exactly what the rest of the grid needs. If Antonelli finishes on the podium again and his rivals stumble, the conversation shifts from title race to title formality. The chasers need a big result this weekend, not a points-finish.
ammo: Antonelli leads the 2026 F1 championship by 41 points heading into Austria
The Lenovo Austrian Grand Prix at the Red Bull Ring is scheduled for June 28, with race start at 9:00 AM EDT
A 41-point gap is roughly equivalent to a win-and-a-half advantage under the current F1 points system
f1.whatToSay: Antonelli is up 41 points — at some point you stop calling it a title race and just call it his championship.

culture.topic: California's Michelin guide just got a major upgrade
culture.whatHappened: The 2026 California Michelin guide elevated Californios and Enclos to three-star status, bumped Kato in Los Angeles to two stars, and handed out first-ever Michelin stars to nine restaurants across the state.
culture.whyItMatters: Two three-star elevations in the same year in the same region is genuinely rare — it signals the culinary bar in California moved, not just shuffled. If you eat out in LA or San Francisco, there's new territory worth exploring before the reservations become impossible.
culture.theRead: Three-star restaurants are rare anywhere in the world. When two places get elevated in the same year in the same region, it's a real signal, not a PR cycle. Kato hitting two stars is the interesting one — LA fine dining has long played second fiddle to San Francisco, so that's a genuine shift in gravity. The nine new one-star spots mean there's probably something good opening near you that's worth trying before it gets impossible to book.
ammo: Californios and Enclos both elevated to three Michelin stars in the 2026 California guide
Kato in Los Angeles earned two Michelin stars
Nine restaurants earned their first Michelin star in the 2026 California guide
culture.whatToSay: California's Michelin guide just reset — if you've been meaning to try somewhere new, this week is the time before it gets impossible to book.

culture.topic: Lionel Richie cuts St. Paul show short after falling ill
culture.whatHappened: Lionel Richie cut his performance short at the Grand Casino Arena in St. Paul after falling ill mid-show. He was co-headlining a tour with Earth, Wind and Fire.
culture.whyItMatters: Richie is 76 and in the middle of a major co-headlining tour — any health scare mid-show is going to raise questions about the rest of the run and whether dates get rescheduled.
culture.theRead: Nobody wants to see a legend leave the stage early, but the bigger question is what this means for the rest of the tour. Earth, Wind and Fire fans who bought tickets for the full co-headlining experience are now watching the schedule closely. Richie has been performing at a high level for decades, so this is likely a one-off — but at 76, any mid-show exit gets attention fast.
ammo: Lionel Richie cut his St. Paul performance short after falling ill
The show was at Grand Casino Arena as part of a co-headlining tour with Earth, Wind and Fire
Richie is 76 years old
culture.whatToSay: Lionel Richie had to cut his St. Paul show short last night — hope it's nothing serious, but that's a tough one for anyone who had tickets.

copy.finalSharpTake: The Rays demolished the Royals 13–2 — Kansas City's offense has gone silent at exactly the wrong time, and Tampa's pitching just reminded everyone why this division stays competitive. Apple dropped 6.1% today while small caps jumped 3.1% on the week — mega-cap tech is paying for months of elevated valuations, and the rotation into cheaper, domestic names is real. Onsemi paying $7 billion for Synaptics is the clearest sign yet that chip consolidation is the strategy now — building from scratch is too slow and too expensive.

glance.sports: Rays 13, Royals 2 · Athletics 9, Giants 6 · Phillies 10, Nationals 5
glance.markets: Onsemi buys Synaptics for $7B · Apple -6.1%, Microsoft -3.5% · Small caps up 3.1% on the week
glance.culture: California Michelin guide adds 9 new stars · Lionel Richie cuts St. Paul show short

theTake.office: Tampa Bay just reminded Kansas City that regular-season baseball rewards depth — the Rays' pitching is quietly the most underrated asset in the AL right now, and a 13–2 win is how you prove it.
theTake.bar: Apple down 6.1% in a single day while small caps are up 3.1% on the week — the rotation is real, and anyone still overweight mega-cap tech is going to have a rough summer.

nhl.headline: Jets at Oilers — No Result Yet
nhl.whyCare1: Winnipeg visits Edmonton at Rogers Place with playoff positioning on the line in a tight Western Conference race.
nhl.whyCare2: Home ice in the playoffs carries real weight — rest, comfort, and the mental edge all shift when you're playing in someone else's building.
nhl.watchFor: Special teams execution. Whoever controls the power play and penalty kill typically controls playoff series.
nhl.theRead: Winnipeg and Edmonton both have the talent to make a Cup run, but one team is about to get real uncomfortable. Whoever steals a win on the road here sends a message that they're not rattled. This is the kind of game where depth and goaltending win it, not skill alone.
ammo: Winnipeg Jets at Edmonton Oilers at Rogers Place, Edmonton
Game has not yet been played — no result available
Western Conference playoff positioning is at stake
nhl.whatToSay: If Edmonton can't protect home ice here, they're in for a long series.

dynamicSportsText.whatHappened: Tampa Bay demolished Kansas City 13–2 on Thursday.
dynamicSportsText.whyItMatters: An 11-run margin is a blowout that signals complete pitching control and offensive dominance. Tampa scored in bunches while Kansas City couldn't generate anything meaningful.
dynamicSportsText.theRead: This is the kind of win that breaks a team's spirit. The Rays came out swinging and didn't let up. Kansas City had no answer at any point. One game doesn't define a season, but when you lose 13–2, the other team's doing everything right and you're doing nothing right.
ammo: Final score: Tampa Bay 13, Kansas City 2
11-run margin of victory
Game played Thursday
dynamicSportsText.whatToBringUp: Tampa Bay just ran Kansas City off the field 13–2—that's the kind of complete dismantling that doesn't happen by accident.
dynamicSportsText.ourPick: Tampa Bay looks dangerous right now because they can score in volume and clearly have pitching that keeps opponents shut down, which is the formula for a playoff team.

stories.headline: Onsemi to buy Synaptics for $7B in all‑stock deal
stories.whatHappened: Onsemi agreed today to acquire Synaptics in a $7 billion all‑stock transaction.
stories.whyItMatters: Massive consolidation in chip sector could reshape AI/hardware supply chains and impact broader tech sentiment.
stories.guytalkRead: This isn’t just another merger — Onsemi grabbing Synaptics for $7B shows how hot chip real estate still is. If you're watching hardware stocks or AI gadget supply chains, this could shift the board.
stories.whatToSay: ‘Onsemi buying Synaptics for $7B — now that’s a hardware flex.’
stories.selectionReason: Huge tech sector deal today with clear implications for AI/hardware conversation.

stories.headline: Starlink offers free internet in Venezuela after quake
stories.whatHappened: Starlink is providing free internet access in Venezuela in response to deadly earthquakes today.
stories.whyItMatters: Connectivity in disaster zones is life‑critical, and this move shows how private space‑based services can plug gaps fast.
stories.guytalkRead: So SpaceX’s Starlink is back in the headlines — not for rockets this time but hooking up post‑quake Venezuela. Tech doing some literal real‑world good, finally.
stories.whatToSay: ‘Starlink just went full rescue mode in Venezuela today.’
stories.selectionReason: Interesting tech/business story with human impact — not typical gadget news.

stories.headline: US moves ahead with $700M jet‑engine sale to Turkey
stories.whatHappened: The US approved a $700 million sale of jet engines to Turkey today, despite lawmakers’ objections.
stories.whyItMatters: This is a significant move in defense relations — impacts NATO dynamics and regional power balances.
stories.guytalkRead: We’re talking jets and geopolitics — $700 million deal with Turkey cleared today. If you care about defence or alliances, this is big.
stories.whatToSay: ‘US just green‑lit a $700M jet‑engine sale to Turkey today.’
stories.selectionReason: Major defense/politics news with real global stakes.

stories.headline: Samsung plans $648B South Korea investment
stories.whatHappened: Samsung unveiled plans today to invest $648 billion in South Korea, including major chip plant expansion.
stories.whyItMatters: It’s one of the largest corporate bets ever — feeds into U.S.‑China tech race and domestic chip supply chains.
stories.guytalkRead: Samsung dropping nearly $650 billion on South Korean manufacturing — basically says ‘we’re still king of chips.’
stories.whatToSay: ‘Samsung just committed $648B at home — chip bets are still on.’
stories.selectionReason: Massive business/tech investment news with broader economic implications.

stories.headline: OpenAI leans toward postponing IPO until next year
stories.whatHappened: OpenAI is reportedly leaning toward delaying its IPO until next year, sources say today.
stories.whyItMatters: Delay in public debut affects AI market valuations and investor expectations right now.
stories.guytalkRead: OpenAI pushing their IPO to 2027 or whatever again? That’s going to keep AI hype charged — for better or worse.
context: IPO likely delayed until next year
stories.whatToSay: ‘Looks like OpenAI is kicking their IPO to next year.’
stories.selectionReason: High‑interest AI/public markets news with huge cultural attention.
stories.verificationConcerns: Based on NYT via Reuters — high confidence but still reported indirect.

stories.whyItMatters: Race fans might be tracking Sonoma action this weekend — this is the kickoff.
stories.guytalkRead: NASCAR guys — Sonoma practice/qualifying are rolling today. Weekend’s opening act is officially on.
context: Confirmed via NASCAR TV schedule Reuters/others
